client_details.disability: T12 complete spinal cord injury resulting from a cycling accident in 2018. Full-time manual wheelchair user with good upper-body strength and trunk control, but no motor or sensory function below the waist. No cognitive impairments; mild neuropathic pain managed with medication.
client_details.name: Daniel Christopher Morales
client_details.grading: USAPA wheelchair classification 2.5 (intermediate high-point offensive player)

delivery_details.delivery_details_line_1: Ship to: Daniel C. Morales, 1428 Willow Creek Drive, Apt 3B, Portland, OR 97219
delivery_details.delivery_details_line_2: Please mark box as “Wheelchair Sports Equipment – Do Not Drop” and leave at front office if recipient not home.
delivery_details.delivery_details_line_3: Preferred shipping carrier: UPS Ground with tracking and signature confirmation.

additional_notes.additional_notes_line_1: Client plays primarily offensive baseline tennis 3–4 times per week on outdoor hard courts; needs a responsive, high-point configuration optimized for quick acceleration, tight turning radius, and aggressive lateral movement.
additional_notes.additional_notes_line_2: Please ensure frame setup allows for minor future adjustments in center of gravity and camber, as the client is still refining his playing style post-injury. Client is right-handed, transfers independently, and loads chair into the back of a Subaru Outback, so ease of wheel removal and manageable overall chair weight are important.
additional_notes.additional_notes_line_3: Client has occasional shoulder overuse soreness (especially right rotator cuff), so minimize propulsion resistance where possible and avoid excessively wide wheelbase. If possible, include printed setup specifications in the box plus a digital copy sent to the email provided for future reference.
